description:
Model a flat roof with specific dimensions and setbacks, determine whether a 'Portrait' or 'Landscape' layout fits more 2m × 1m panels, and save the model with the winning configuration.

Instructions:
1. Draw a rectangular building footprint: 6m (East-West) × 5m (North-South) and extrude it to any height to create a flat-roofed building.
2. On the top roof face, create a 0.5m (500mm) setback from all edges using the Offset tool. The resulting usable inner area should be 5m × 4m.
3. Open Skelion and configure a 'User Defined' panel: Width = 2000mm, Height = 1000mm. Set Row/Col Spacing to 0mm (zero gap), and Tilt to 0°.
4. Test inserting the panels on the inner usable face in both Portrait and Landscape orientations to see which fits more panels (one fits 10, the other fits fewer).
5. Keep the layout that yields the HIGHEST panel count.
6. Save the final model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\optimized_layout.skp'.
